note: Lee et al. Immunity 2010 — we do NOT claim novelty of mTORC2 T-cell biology
Charbonnier et al. Nat Immunol 2015 — motivates the cell-type-conflict safety concern
Benavides-Serrato et al. PLoS One 2017; retracted 2023 (figure-integrity); not cited as support
Ai et al. Rheumatology 2025; retracted 2025 (data miscalculation; human-efficacy figure); not cited as support
Koranova et al. Pharmacol Rep 2024 — undermines JR-AB2-011 as a RICTOR target-engagement tool

finding: RICTOR-specific KD in primary human CD4 T cells reverses a donor-paired inflamed-joint activated-memory programme (tissue-retention + cytotoxic-effector genes) without detectable Treg-identity collapse in this dataset
note: computational mechanism hypothesis, NOT functional validation
